name: updrytwist-updrytwist

metadata.name: updrytwist-UpDryTwist
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)

documentation:
This project is a collection of the utilities that I use all the time in different projects,
most of which are around home automation-type activities, or kiosk screens, or tinkering
with a Raspberry Pi.  

How to Install
--------------
    pip install updrytwist

How to Use
----------

Maybe at some point this will be filled in.  But for now, just read the source.

Contact Information
-------------------

Please contact through github (UpDryTwist).

functions.name: updrytwist.mqttdriver.prepare_mqtt_safe_loop
documentation:
Under Windows, need to change the event loop type.  Call this before
starting your main event loop!
MQTT_LOGGER = logging.getLogger('mqtt')
MQTT_LOGGER.setLevel(logging.INFO)

classes.name: updrytwist.mqttdriver.MqttDriver
documentation: MQTT Driver class for handling MQTT messages.

modules.name: updrytwist
documentation: Copyright (c) 2024. All rights reserved.

modules.name: updrytwist.command_to_config
documentation:
Provides classes and methods to convert command line arguments to a configuration
dictionary, as well as to read values from environment variables and convert them
to a configuration dictionary
Copyright (c) 2024. All rights reserved.

functions.name: updrytwist.command_to_config.merge_dictionaries
documentation: Merges two dictionaries, with dict2 taking precedence over dict1

functions.name: updrytwist.command_to_config.arguments_to_dict
documentation:
Converts command line arguments to a configuration dictionary.  Command line
values are of the form --config_block_block_key=value or --config_block_block_key
value or block_block_key=value.

functions.name: updrytwist.command_to_config.command_line_as_config_dictionary
documentation:
Converts command line arguments to a configuration dictionary.  Command line
values are of the form --config_block_block_key=value or --config_block_block_key
value or block_block_key=value.

functions.name: updrytwist.command_to_config.environment_variables_as_config_dictionary
documentation:
Converts environment variables to a configuration dictionary.  Environment
variables are of the form BLOCK_BLOCK_KEY=value.  If environment_variable_prefix is
provided, then we only consider environment variables that start with that prefix.

classes.name: updrytwist.tkasync.ThreadedClient

methods.name: updrytwist.tkasync.ThreadedClient.__init__
documentation:
Start the GUI and the asynchronous threads. We are in the main
(original) thread of the application, which will later be used by
the GUI as well. We spawn a new thread for the worker (I/O).

methods.name: updrytwist.tkasync.ThreadedClient.periodicCall
documentation: Check every n-many ms if there is something new in the queue.

methods.name: updrytwist.tkasync.ThreadedClient.workerThread1
documentation:
This is where we handle the asynchronous I/O. For example, it may be
a 'select(  )'. One important thing to remember is that the thread has
to yield control pretty regularly, by select or otherwise.

Launch the main part of the GUI and the worker thread. periodicCall and
endApplication could reside in the GUI part, but putting them here
means that you have all the thread controls in a single place.

modules.name: updrytwist.config
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
Copyright (c) 2024. All rights reserved.

functions.name: updrytwist.config.var_to_bool
documentation:
Convert a variable to a boolean value.  Handle expectable values, such as yes,
true, on, and so forth.  Non-zero integers are true. Raises an exception if none
of the above.

functions.name: updrytwist.config.str_to_bool
documentation:
Convert a string to a boolean value.  Handle expectable values, such as yes,
true, on, and so forth.  Non-zero integers are true. Raise an exception if can't
interpret the string.

classes.name: updrytwist.config.CannedConfig
documentation: Used to create a canned configuration that can be passed around.  Mostly for unit testing.
